---
title: "Fig1. Quantifiaction of mRNA in nocodazole treated embryos `r format(Sys.Date(), '%Y-%m-%d')`"
author: "Naly Torres"
date: "25/04/2026"
output:
  word_document: default
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
---




```{r echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, include=FALSE}

library(png)
library(knitr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggsignif)
library(svglite)
library(ggridges)
library(scales)
library(readr)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(tools)
library(tidyverse)
library(dunn.test)




```

### Part 1 ### 

```{r}

# Parent directory containing CSV files for all conditions
parent_directory <- "/Users/nalytorres/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-Colostate/OnishLab - NalyTorres/00_2026_ERM1_Torres_et_al/06_GitHub/Fig1_nocodazole/Fig.1E_nocodazole_total_abundance"


# Function to read CSV, replace Image ID with CSV name, and modify column names
read_modify_csv <- function(file_path, subdirectory) {
  my_df <- read.csv(file_path)
  my_df$Image_ID <- basename(file_path)
  my_df$subdirectory <- subdirectory
  my_df <- my_df[, colnames(my_df) != "X"]
  current_colnames <- colnames(my_df)
  new_colnames <- gsub("\\.", " ", current_colnames)
  colnames(my_df) <- new_colnames
  return(my_df)
}

# List all subdirectories (conditions) in the parent directory
subdirectories <- list.dirs(parent_directory, full.names = FALSE, recursive = FALSE)

# Initialize an empty list to store data frames
all_data <- list()

# Loop through subdirectories (conditions)
for (subdirectory in subdirectories) {
  # List all CSV files in the current subdirectory
  csv_files <- list.files(path = file.path(parent_directory, subdirectory), pattern = "*.csv", full.names = TRUE)
  
  # Loop through CSV files, read data, replace Image ID, and modify column names
  for (file_path in csv_files) {
    current_data <- read_modify_csv(file_path, subdirectory)
    all_data[[file_path]] <- current_data
  }
}

# Combine all data frames into one data frame
combined_data <- bind_rows(all_data)

str(combined_data)
```


```{r}
# Function to read CSV, replace Image ID with CSV name, and modify column names
read_modify_csv <- function(file_path, subdirectory) {
  my_df <- read.csv(file_path)
  my_df$Image_ID <- basename(file_path)
  my_df$subdirectory <- subdirectory
  my_df <- my_df[, colnames(my_df) != "X"]
  current_colnames <- colnames(my_df)
  new_colnames <- gsub("\\.", " ", current_colnames)
  colnames(my_df) <- new_colnames
  
  # Ensure Image_ID is treated as a character column
  my_df$Image_ID <- as.character(my_df$Image_ID)
  
  return(my_df)
}

# List all subdirectories (conditions) in the parent directory
subdirectories <- list.dirs(parent_directory, full.names = FALSE, recursive = FALSE)

# Initialize an empty list to store data frames
all_data <- list()

# Loop through subdirectories (conditions)
for (subdirectory in subdirectories) {
  # List all CSV files in the current subdirectory
  csv_files <- list.files(path = file.path(parent_directory, subdirectory), pattern = "*.csv", full.names = TRUE)
  
  # Loop through CSV files, read data, replace Image ID, and modify column names
  for (file_path in csv_files) {
    current_data <- read_modify_csv(file_path, subdirectory)
    all_data[[file_path]] <- current_data
  }
}

# Combine all data frames into one data frame
combined_data <- bind_rows(all_data)

# View the combined data
combined_data


write_csv(combined_data, file.path(parent_directory, "raw_spot_counts_nocodazole_treatment.csv"))
```



```{r}


df <- combined_data %>%
  rename(
    set3_mRNA = `set 3_mRNA total molecules`,
    erm1_mRNA = `erm 1_mRNA total molecules`
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    nocodazole = recode(
      subdirectory,
      "0uM_nocodazole_total_abundance"   = "0 µM Nocodazole",
      "150uM_nocodazole_total_abundance" = "150 µM Nocodazole"
    )
  )

df_long <- df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(set3_mRNA, erm1_mRNA),
    names_to = "mRNA",
    values_to = "total_molecules"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    mRNA = factor(mRNA, levels = c("set3_mRNA", "erm1_mRNA")),
    nocodazole = factor(nocodazole,
                        levels = c("0 µM Nocodazole", "150 µM Nocodazole"))
  )




library(ggpubr)

# Create plot with violin + box
p <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = nocodazole, y = total_molecules, fill = nocodazole)) +
  geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, fill = "white", outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ mRNA, scales = "fixed",
             labeller = as_labeller(c(
               set3_mRNA = "set-3 mRNA",
               erm1_mRNA = "erm-1 mRNA"
             ))) +
  theme_classic() +
  scale_fill_manual(
  values = c(
    "0 µM Nocodazole"   = "grey70", 
    "150 µM Nocodazole"  = "lightblue")
) +
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = "Total mRNA molecules",
    title = "erm-1 mRNA abundance ± nocodazole"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
  axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", angle = 15, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")
  )



unique(df$subdirectory)
df_long %>%
  count(mRNA, nocodazole)

ggsave(
  filename = "erm1_set3_mRNA_nocodazole_plot.svg",
  plot = p,
  width = 6,
  height = 6
)

p

```

```{r}
summary_stats <- df_long %>%
  group_by(mRNA, nocodazole) %>%
  summarise(
    mean = mean(total_molecules, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd = sd(total_molecules, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),
    sem = sd / sqrt(n),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

summary_stats

write.csv(summary_stats, file.path(parent_directory, "erm1_set3_mRNA_summary_stats.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
```


Doing Wilcox here because there are only two groups. 
```{r}
library(broom)
library(dplyr)

erm1_test <- wilcox.test(
  `erm 1_mRNA total molecules` ~ subdirectory,
  data = combined_data,
  exact = FALSE
)

set3_test <- wilcox.test(
  `set 3_mRNA total molecules` ~ subdirectory,
  data = combined_data,
  exact = FALSE
)

stats_table <- bind_rows(
  tidy(erm1_test) %>% mutate(gene = "erm-1 mRNA", .before = 1),
  tidy(set3_test) %>% mutate(gene = "set-3 mRNA", .before = 1)
) %>%
  select(gene, statistic, p.value, method, alternative)

stats_table

# Save for metadata
write.csv(stats_table, file.path(parent_directory, "wilcoxon_stats_table.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
```
